authoritative_sources.title: RFC 1034 - Domain Names - Concepts and Facilities
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IETF protocol standard defining DNS hierarchy, zones, delegation, caching, and resolver concepts that constrain DNS architecture and troubleshooting claims.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: DNS intake, zone design, delegation review, resolver boundary assessment, fault isolation

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 1035 - Domain Names - Implementation and Specification
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IETF protocol standard for DNS messages, resource records, query behavior, and response interpretation used to read DNS artifacts.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: record review, zone-file evidence review, query and response troubleshooting
practical_use_in_pack: output fidelity for architecture and fault-isolation notes

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 8499 - DNS Terminology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IETF terminology reference for consistent DNS terms used to avoid over-broad or ambiguous ownership claims.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: intake classification, artifact naming, handoff notes, evaluation wording

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 2131 - Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IETF DHCPv4 protocol standard for address leasing, allocation lifecycle, client/server exchange, and lease-state interpretation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: DHCP scope planning, lease-policy review, conflict troubleshooting, evidence review
practical_use_in_pack: DHCP scope and lease scenario constraints

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 8415 - Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol for IPv6
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IETF DHCPv6 standard required for IPv6 addressing, options, and lease behavior in dual-stack planning.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: IPv6 planning, DHCPv6/SLAAC tradeoff review, dual-stack evidence review

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 8156 - DHCP Failover Protocol
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IETF DHCP failover reference for high-availability behavior, partner states, and recovery limits in DHCP planning.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: availability planning, DHCP failover review, incident recovery sequencing

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 2317 - Classless IN-ADDR.ARPA Delegation
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IETF reference for reverse DNS delegation of classless IPv4 blocks, which governs PTR remediation in fragmented subnets.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: reverse DNS planning, PTR ownership review, audit remediation

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 4033 and RFC 4035 - DNSSEC Requirements and Protocol Modifications
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IETF DNSSEC references for trust, authentication, resolver validation, and signed response behavior needed for DNSSEC rollout guidance.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: DNSSEC readiness, key rollover planning, resolver validation review, availability tradeoff assessment

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-81 Rev. 2 - Secure Domain Name System (DNS) Deployment Guide
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST official DNS security deployment guidance for secure DNS architecture, operational practices, and DNS failure handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: secure DNS design, DNSSEC planning, resolver policy, audit evidence, failure handling
practical_use_in_pack: boundary between hardening guidance and security attestation

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5 - Security and Privacy Controls for Information Systems and Organizations
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST official control catalog for inventory, configuration management, audit, monitoring, contingency, and access-control evidence language; used for evidence expectations, not certification.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: evidence mapping, audit package assembly, control-context caveats, retention and owner review

authoritative_sources.title: Encrypted DNS Implementation Guidance
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CISA public enterprise guidance for encrypted DNS deployment considerations, protective DNS posture, and resolver policy impacts.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: resolver policy review, DNS security hardening, monitoring caveats, adjacent security escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Amazon VPC IP Address Manager
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AWS official cloud IPAM documentation for address planning, allocation, utilization, monitoring, and governance in AWS VPC environments.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: cloud IPAM intake, prefix allocation, capacity review, address exhaustion handling

authoritative_sources.title: DHCP option sets in Amazon VPC
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AWS official VPC guidance for DHCP option sets and resolver behavior in AWS tenant networks.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: cloud DHCP review, resolver option review, tenant platform adaptation
practical_use_in_pack: DHCP option evidence in cloud networks

authoritative_sources.title: Subnet sizing for IPv4
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AWS official subnet sizing guidance for reserved addresses and practical address allocation constraints in cloud VPCs.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: resource allocation, subnet planning, capacity and exhaustion assessment

authoritative_sources.title: DNS Architecture in Windows Server
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Microsoft official Windows Server DNS documentation for DNS architecture, records, dynamic updates, and platform integration in Windows estates.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Windows DNS evidence review, DNS record analysis, tenant platform adaptation

authoritative_sources.title: Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol in Windows Server
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Microsoft official Windows Server DHCP documentation for DHCP role behavior, scopes, options, leases, and operational records in Windows environments.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Windows DHCP evidence review, scope planning, lease troubleshooting
practical_use_in_pack: DHCP scope and lease record examples

authoritative_sources.title: IP Address Management in Windows Server
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Microsoft official Windows Server IPAM documentation for IP address space management, DNS/DHCP integration, and IPAM evidence in Windows environments.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: IPAM source-of-truth review, address lifecycle, ownership and audit evidence

authoritative_sources.title: NetBox IPAM
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official NetBox documentation for a widely used IPAM source-of-truth model covering prefixes, IP addresses, VLANs, VRFs, and allocation workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: IPAM data modeling, source-of-truth mapping, prefix allocation, audit evidence

authoritative_sources.title: Current package task baseline from dns-dhcp-and-ipam-specialist
authoritative_sources.source_class: Current package task baseline from dns-dhcp-and-ipam-specialist
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Committed package baseline used to preserve existing specialist boundary, supported tasks, common inputs, output shapes, and legacy evaluation rigor while treating the refreshed spec as canonical.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: baseline comparison, regression coverage, boundary preservation

workflow_stages.name: Demand or work intake for DDI planning
workflow_stages.description: Classify the request as DNS, DHCP, IPAM, reverse DNS, DNSSEC, capacity, troubleshooting, evidence, or adjacent-lane work.

workflow_stages.name: Source and record selection
workflow_stages.description: Select current IETF, NIST, CISA, vendor, platform, and tenant records before making workflow claims.
required_evidence: zone, scope, lease, prefix, telemetry, and change records

workflow_stages.name: Constraint and capacity assessment
workflow_stages.description: Assess address pools, resolver capacity, authoritative service posture, change windows, validation capacity, priority rules, and tenant constraints.

workflow_stages.name: Schedule, priority, or allocation recommendation support
workflow_stages.description: Produce advisory schedule, allocation, migration, rollback, and remediation recommendations without final approval claims.

workflow_stages.name: Fault isolation and failure handling
workflow_stages.description: Separate DDI failures from routing, firewall, endpoint, identity, application, and monitoring failures while requesting targeted evidence.

workflow_stages.name: Evidence and audit packaging
workflow_stages.description: Produce traceable DDI evidence packages with owner, source, retention, caveat, and non-attestation boundaries.

workflow_stages.name: Refresh and exception escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when sources, tenant policies, safety constraints, priority rules, evidence, or approval authority are missing or conflicting.

artifacts: DNS zone files, hosted-zone inventory, registrar and delegation records, resolver and forwarding topology, DNSSEC state, DS records, query logs, and reverse-zone ownership
DHCP scope, pool, exclusion, option, reservation, relay, lease, conflict, failover, and utilization datasets
IPAM prefixes, aggregates, VRFs, address lifecycle states, duplicate/orphan records, owner assignments, approval records, and audit history
incident timelines, resolver metrics, DHCP server logs, latency/error telemetry, packet evidence, alert history, and outage evidence
change tickets, maintenance windows, change-freeze rules, business criticality, priority rules, safety or regulated-workload constraints, and approval matrix
schedule or allocation recommendation, constraint log, migration plan, rollback plan, fault-isolation note, evidence package, and orchestrator handoff

examples: tenant CMDB or network source of truth

systems_of_record.system: Change, approval, policy, and industry-specific record systems

allowed: Recommend DNS zone, delegation, resolver, forwarding, reverse DNS, DNSSEC readiness, and validation approaches.
Recommend DHCP scope, lease, option, reservation, relay, failover, and DHCPv6/SLAAC coexistence approaches.
Recommend IPAM data model, prefix hierarchy, subnet allocation, exhaustion response, conflict remediation, and audit evidence improvements.
Prepare schedule or allocation recommendations, constraint logs, risk-ranked options, rollback criteria, and approval-needed notes.
Request targeted evidence and state confidence when troubleshooting DDI symptoms.
escalate: Demand exceeds available address, resolver, DHCP, validation, change-window, or staffing capacity and tradeoffs need approval.
Conflicting priorities lack an approved decision rule.
Tenant policy, public standards, vendor guidance, contract terms, regulatory obligations, safety rules, or change authority conflict.
Required topology, DNS, DHCP, IPAM, telemetry, owner, retention, source-precedence, or approval facts are missing.
The request requires final production, budget, staffing, legal, compliance, security, safety, or risk authority.
refuse: Endpoint patching, MDM policy, identity directory administration, PAM policy, MFA rollout, or endpoint registry changes.
Application-layer routing logic, API design, service-discovery code, application implementation, or application-only debugging.
Live firewall rule deployment, perimeter tuning, exploit execution, vulnerability remediation, forensic investigation, or broad security architecture.
Replacing platform networking ownership outside DNS, DHCP, reverse DNS, and address management.
Presenting a planning recommendation as final budget, staffing, operational, compliance, legal, risk, or production approval.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Generic DNS advice ignores authoritative versus recursive boundaries.
domain_failure_modes.impact: Resolver loops, cache poisoning exposure, split-horizon leakage, unresolved delegation, and unclear ownership.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: DHCP capacity or failover is planned without lease, scope, reservation, relay, and renewal evidence.
domain_failure_modes.impact: Address exhaustion, duplicate assignment, branch outage, misleading routing diagnosis, and uncontrolled emergency changes.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: IPAM source-of-truth records appear complete but omit owner, lifecycle state, conflict, reservation, or tenant priority facts.
domain_failure_modes.impact: Duplicate allocations, stale records, unsupported schedule decisions, and audit gaps.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Reverse DNS and DNSSEC are treated as routine record updates without delegation, chain-of-trust, resolver, validation, or rollback constraints.
domain_failure_modes.impact: PTR inconsistency, validation failures, signed-zone outages, and hard-to-rollback name resolution failures.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Cross-industry tenant constraints are treated as cosmetic.
domain_failure_modes.impact: Recommendations ignore change freezes, safety-sensitive workloads, regulated retention, business criticality, or tenant approval authority.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Adjacent endpoint, identity, application, firewall, security, or approval work is absorbed by the DDI specialist.
domain_failure_modes.impact: Boundary drift, unauthorized execution, wrong owner routing, compliance risk, and unsupported operational claims.

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: Conflicting standards, vendor guidance, tenant policy, or safety facts are synthesized without precedence authority.
domain_failure_modes.impact: Over-confident recommendations that violate tenant policy, platform limits, or safety/change authority.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Conflicting priorities with no approved decision rule
Request for final approval outside lane authority
Normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions

required_workflow_questions.question: What named-industry records and systems actually govern the lane?
required_workflow_questions.answer: For cross-industry DDI, tenant-specific topology, DNS, DHCP, IPAM, monitoring, incident, change, policy, criticality, retention, safety, and approval systems govern safe recommendations. Public sources constrain protocol and platform claims, but tenant systems determine ownership, timing, and authority.

required_workflow_questions.question: What failure modes make this industry materially different from a generic function?
required_workflow_questions.answer: Cross-industry DDI is materially changed by tenant-specific criticality, regulated workloads, change freezes, safety windows, retention policy, cloud/on-prem platform records, and source-precedence rules. The pack must ask for those facts instead of issuing generic DNS/DHCP/IPAM best practices.

required_workflow_questions.question: What adjacent authority boundaries constrain the lane?
required_workflow_questions.answer: Endpoint, identity, application, firewall, routing/switching, broad security, legal, compliance, budget, staffing, final production approval, and risk acceptance remain outside the lane. The specialist may preserve DDI evidence for handoff but must not own those decisions.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant source precedence between vendor guidance, public standards, and internal policy
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: At runtime, request tenant policy and approval authority. If conflict remains, return to orchestrator instead of synthesizing a confident recommendation.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Sector-specific safety, privacy, retention, or regulated-change constraints for a cross-industry tenant
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: At runtime, require the named tenant industry record set and safety/change authority. If absent, use dnsipam-17 or dnsipam-18 escalation behavior.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Exact platform behavior for tenant-specific DNS, DHCP, or IPAM products not represented in the source baseline
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Request current official vendor documentation and tenant configuration exports before making provider-specific claims.

notes: Research output constrains the spec pack to DNS, DHCP, reverse DNS, DNSSEC, and IPAM advisory work with planning, scheduling, allocation, evidence, and boundary controls.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated spec pack because unresolved tenant-specific ambiguity is converted into runtime retrieval and escalation rules.
The current package baseline was used for continuity only; the refreshed spec pack is the canonical source of truth.
